name: The Citizens Bank
description_short: The Citizens Bank is a community bank serving South Carolina with 18 branch locations offering checking, savings, and online banking services, FDIC-insured.
description_long:
The Citizens Bank is a regional community bank headquartered in Turbeville, South Carolina, with its home office in Olanta. The bank has expanded to 18 branch locations across South Carolina, including recent openings in Conway, with branches in communities such as Timmonsville, Sumter, Lake City, Pawleys Island, Florence, Georgetown, and Camden. The bank emphasizes its role as a community-focused financial institution actively engaged in the areas it serves.

The Citizens Bank offers traditional banking products including checking and savings accounts, online banking through their digital platform (accessible via username login), and a mobile app called TCB 2GO. The bank provides standard banking hours (Monday-Thursday 8:30 a.m. to 5:00 p.m., Friday 8:30 a.m. to 5:30 p.m.) at most locations with both lobby and drive-thru services. Customers have the option to receive printed or emailed receipts as of April 7th. The bank also provides fraud and security resources, including security awareness pages and check-washing fraud information.

What distinguishes The Citizens Bank is its emphasis on community banking and local branch presence across rural and mid-sized South Carolina communities. The bank maintains named branch managers and area executives at each location, suggesting personalized customer service. The institution is FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. The bank actively maintains social media presence on LinkedIn, Facebook, X (Twitter), and Instagram, and provides security awareness education to customers.

As a traditional regional bank, The Citizens Bank appears to serve customers seeking conventional banking services in South Carolina communities. However, the website provides limited detail on specific account features, fees, interest rates, or loan products beyond the basic mention of checking and savings offerings. Customers considering this bank should contact branches directly for detailed product information, rate comparisons, and fee structures.
services: Online banking platform with secure login
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
18 branch locations across South Carolina with extended hours including Friday evening service until 5:30 p.m.
Named branch managers and area executives at each location for personalized service
Online banking platform with username login and mobile TCB 2GO app available
Emailed receipt option available as of April 7th for digital record-keeping
Active community engagement and social media presence (LinkedIn, Facebook, X, Instagram)
Fraud and security resources including security awareness page and check-washing fraud information
cons: Website lacks specific information on account fees, minimum balances, or interest rates for checking/savings products
Limited details on loan products, credit cards, or mortgage services beyond basic savings/checking mention
No information provided about mobile banking features, bill pay capabilities, or ATM network availability
Geographic limitation to South Carolina only may not serve customers in other states
Temporary driving certificates explicitly not accepted as valid identification, limiting options for some customers
best_for: South Carolina residents seeking community banking with local branch presence and personalized service
Customers in rural and mid-sized SC communities where national banks have limited branch coverage
Depositors prioritizing FDIC insurance and traditional banking services over digital-only options
diagnosis: The Citizens Bank is best for South Carolina residents who value community banking and local branch relationships over digital-first banking or specialized financial products. The primary caveat is that the website provides insufficient detail on account features, fees, rates, and full service offerings—prospective customers must contact branches directly for detailed product comparisons.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
